body.title: [sprint] Corpus visibility: capture warm/intercept hits + add role/turn labels to dispatch records
body.description:
**Problem:** the every-byte I/O corpus cannot show the starve-curve. (a) Warm hits BYPASS capture: `try_intercept` returns in device.py (~432-437) BEFORE `_capture_io` fires, so a cache/intercept hit produces NO corpus record — the curve's numerator is invisible. (b) Dispatch records carry no role/turn label; architect vs editor is only recoverable by grepping system-prompt text, so the curve can't be segmented by layer.

**Proposed shape:** (1) emit a corpus record on the warm/intercept path — `outcome='warm'`, the served answer, provenance. (2) Add `role` (architect/editor/critic) + `turn` (+ optional `parent_id`) to InferenceRequest and the corpus record; populate from the coding loop.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/device.py (call _capture_io on the intercept path), io_corpus.py (record fields), shim.py / InferenceRequest (role/turn/parent fields), architect_editor.py + agentic_loop.py (populate role/turn).

**⚠️ HIGH-inertia pre-approval (Akien, 2026-07-05):** additive instrumentation to the inference design center (device.py, agentic_loop.py) is APPROVED. This is warm-hit capture + role/turn fields, not a rewrite.

**Design rules:** fail-soft capture (a corpus write must NEVER break dispatch); log the crossing; one homogeneous capture point.

**Scope boundary:** warm-hit capture + role/turn labels ONLY. NOT the question-equivalence decision log, NOT nexus demography.

**Completion criteria:** a dispatch on the intercept/warm path writes a corpus record with outcome='warm' (today: zero); every dispatch record carries role + turn.

**Test plan:** hermetic tests/inference/... — a dispatch forced down the intercept path produces a corpus record with outcome='warm'; a coding-loop dispatch carries role='architect', turn=N. PROOF NODE: the warm-path dispatch produces a corpus record where today's code writes nothing. red→green via proof_emitter.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
